C:\Users\nilay\AppData\Roaming\npm\copilot.cmd --model gpt-5.4 --log-dir Y:\.sources\localm-tuts\courses\_examples\ctx-sdlc\lessons\08-operating-model\.output\logs --log-level debug --stream off --share Y:\.sources\localm-tuts\courses\_examples\ctx-sdlc\lessons\08-operating-model\.output\logs\session.md --add-dir Y:\.sources\localm-tuts\courses\_examples\ctx-sdlc\lessons\08-operating-model\src --allow-all-tools --allow-all-paths --no-ask-user --deny-tool=powershell --deny-tool=sql -p Read .github/copilot-instructions.md, .github/scripts/audit_context.py, .github/scripts/detect_stale_refs.py, .github/examples/clean/copilot-instructions.md, .github/examples/drifted/copilot-instructions.md, and docs/maintenance-schedule.md. Produce a read-only operating-model analysis for context maintenance. Return: summary, what kinds of drift the lesson is trying to catch, the most dangerous differences between the clean and drifted examples, one false positive, one hard negative, a maintenance cadence recommendation, and prioritized fixes. Explicitly call out copy-paste drift, stale references, contradictory rules, over-specification, and under-specification. Do not modify files, do not run shell commands, and do not use SQL or any other write-capable tools. Inspect and read only.
